Mary.name: Mary
Mary.bio: Creative arts teacher. Guides our youngest students through hands-on projects.

Caitlin.name: Caitlin
Caitlin.bio: Dance, writing, and making. She's the teacher every kid wants to spend the afternoon with.

Dan.name: Dan
Dan.bio: Founder and principal. Teaches the classes he loves — music, philosophy, history, entrepreneurship.

Jerome.name: Jerome
Jerome.bio: P.E., drill, chess, and logic. Brings structure, discipline, and a good game.

Luke.name: Luke
Luke.bio: Music, movement, and maker. Teaches rhythm, 3D modeling, and Christianity.

Jordan.name: Jordan
Jordan.bio: Coding, drawing, and AI. Shows kids how to build with tools old and new.

Phil.name: Phil
Phil.bio: Robotics co-teacher. Patient, precise, and great with hands-on builders.

Jordan/Phil.name: Jordan & Phil
Jordan/Phil.bio: Our robotics duo. Jordan on the code side, Phil on the build side.

Phil/Jordan.name: Phil & Jordan
Phil/Jordan.bio: Our robotics duo. Phil on the build side, Jordan on the code side.

Phil/Mary.name: Phil & Mary
Phil/Mary.bio: Co-teaching younger robotics — gentle on-ramp for first-time builders.

descriptions.Perler Beads: Colorful bead patterns on pegboards, then ironed to keep. Great focus and fine motor work.
descriptions.Clay Sculpt: Hands in clay. Build a small creature, a bowl, or whatever you imagine.
descriptions.Paint / Paper Craft: Acrylics, watercolor, and paper. Make something to take home.
descriptions.Lego / Minecraft Fort: Build a fort or a world. Legos in real life, Minecraft on screen.
descriptions.Legos & Perler: A two-in-one afternoon of classic building toys.
descriptions.Drawing: Basic sketching — proportions, shading, and how to see like an artist.
descriptions.Drill / P.E. Games: Organized team games, drills, and movement. Fun but structured.
Organized movement and team games, a little more focused than the younger version.
descriptions.Rhythm: Clapping, drumming, and body percussion. Music without instruments.
descriptions.Dance: Movement basics — rhythm, body awareness, and choreography.
descriptions.Choir: Sing together. Harmony, dynamics, and how to use your voice.
descriptions.Song Writing: Write your own song — lyrics, melody, and structure.
descriptions.Creative Writing: Short stories, poems, and getting past the blank page.
descriptions.Public Speaking: How to stand, how to breathe, how to say what you mean.
descriptions.Debate: Argue both sides of something that matters. Think on your feet.
descriptions.Critical Thinking: Logic puzzles, fallacies, and how to actually reason about hard questions.
descriptions.Mindset: Growth mindset in practice — how the best athletes, artists, and builders think.
descriptions.World History: Big-picture history — turning points, not memorization.
descriptions.Political Science: How governments work. How they should work. Discussion-driven.
descriptions.Entrepreneurship: How businesses start. Pitch an idea, stress-test it, iterate.
descriptions.Chess: Learn or level up. Tactics, openings, and actual play.
descriptions.Geometry / Logic: Geometric reasoning plus logic puzzles that sharpen the mind.
descriptions.Robotics: Build and program a robot. Teams work in parallel.
descriptions.Coding: Write real code. We start simple, move fast.
descriptions.3D Modeling: Design an object in 3D software. Ready to print if time allows.
descriptions.A.I. LLM: What large language models actually are, and how to use them well.
descriptions.A.I. Vibe Code: Build software by describing it to an AI. The new way.
descriptions.Christianity: Faith basics — the story, the questions, the honest answers.
